justification: Both domain rules (state transition invariant and refund precondition) are present with detailed testable_assertions. All three architecture decisions (PATCH endpoint, OrderStateMachine, RefundService contract) are present with implementation_notes — exceeds the minimum of two. acceptance_criteria preserves ORDER_STATE_INVALID verbatim in the first criterion. terminology_alignment is present and addresses Bird/MJ language differences across three entries. task_context is one specific, actionable paragraph. No invented information — all details trace back to the described scenario. Open questions are appropriately flagged rather than filled with invented answers.

observations.text: Three architecture decisions instead of the required two, with the OrderStateMachine separated as its own decision — adds clarity for Shaq
terminology_alignment covers three terms including the subtle 'revert-to-pending' phrasing from Bird
Open questions are well-scoped and flag genuine ambiguities (RefundService contract shape, full transition table, HTTP status code) rather than inventing answers
contradictions_resolved is empty rather than fabricated, showing discipline

terminology_alignment does not explicitly reconcile Bird's 'order status' with MJ's 'state' — the rubric specifically calls out that Bird says SHIPPED and MJ says PATCH /orders/{id}/state and Magic should confirm these refer to the same concept. The entries address related terms but miss this exact reconciliation.
acceptance_criteria hedges on HTTP status (422 or 400) rather than committing — minor imprecision but the error code itself is preserved
